location.name: Vaur Tessek
location.weather: salt-fog thick on the water each morning

crime.time_of_death: Between the second bell of night and first light
crime.motive: Silencing a witness to an old crime

characters.name: Tessek Oravi
characters.description: A meticulous woman of about fifty, lineage bookkeeper for the Tessek family and a respected salt-merchant in her own right. She is found slumped against the railing of the salt-pan platform, her posture too composed for a fall — and her outer robe, despite the heavy pre-dawn fog, is dry.

characters.name: Maret Doun
characters.description: The Tessek salt-works overseer, a sturdy woman of about forty who speaks in the measured tones of someone used to giving direction. She is the one who found the body and sent the overnight runner for help, and she answers questions about it with a steadiness that sits just at the edge of composure.

characters.name: Nitha Tessek
characters.description: The Matron of the Tessek lineage, a sharp-faced woman of sixty who carries her authority the way others carry grief — always present, always shaping how she moves through a room. She makes no secret of the fact that she and Oravi had clashed repeatedly over lineage records, and she speaks of the death with genuine shock and unconcealed relief.

characters.name: Senne Arak
characters.description: The Matron's night-watcher, a young woman of about twenty-five who stands at her post with the careful stillness of someone trying not to be noticed. She is quick to speak but her answers arrive in the slightly-too-complete way of someone who has rehearsed them.

characters.name: Dauvek Pol
characters.description: Tessek Oravi's nephew — her sister's son — a broad-shouldered man of about thirty-five who trades at the covered market in display salts and cured goods. He does not hide that he had been pressing his aunt to have a lineage document revised in his favor, and he speaks of her with the brittle warmth of a man who has been recently refused.

characters.name: Irret Sou
characters.description: A traveling salt-auditor from an inland trade council, a composed woman of thirty who has been berthed at the quayside for three days conducting a routine cargo review. She was on deck before dawn for the morning muster and was the first outsider to reach the body; she is forthcoming with what she observed and speaks with the precision of someone accustomed to recording exactly what she sees.

items.name: Salt-Scraper
items.description: A narrow iron-bladed tool, roughly palm-length, used to lift caked salt from the harvest pans; the blade is clean and the handle wood is pale and unweathered, showing none of the grey salt-staining common to tools in regular use.

items.name: Records Room Key
items.description: A heavy iron key with a Tessek lineage mark filed into the bow; the shank shows no tarnish, and a faint damp smell clings to the metal.

items.name: Salt Ledger
items.description: A thick bound ledger lying open to a page dense with columns of figures; the ink in the rightmost column of the open page is smeared in a broad streak, as though the page was disturbed before the ink had dried.

items.name: Watch-Log Tablet
items.description: A wooden tablet carved with rows of notched marks recording check-in intervals; the notches on one side of a blank gap are pressed deeper into the wood than those on the other side.

locations.name: The Quayside
locations.flavour: Flat-bottomed trade boats rock against salt-bleached pilings as the morning fog lifts in slow ribbons off the estuary water.

locations.name: The Covered Market
locations.flavour: Bolts of undyed canvas overhead filter the pale autumn light into something softer, and the air holds the mingled smell of brine, clay salt, and lamp oil.

locations.name: The Records Room
locations.flavour: Shelves of bound ledgers and rolled documents line every wall, and the single lamp bracket above the reading table has left a ghost of soot on the plaster ceiling.

locations.name: The Matron's House
locations.flavour: The main hall smells of cedar oil and old salt, and the Tessek lineage marks above the door lintel have been repainted so many times the original lines are lost beneath them.

locations.name: The Upper Walkway
locations.flavour: A covered passage of salt-weathered planks runs between the Matron's House and the works complex, its boards damp and filmed with the fine white residue that settles everywhere in Vaur Tessek.

locations.name: The Salt-Works
locations.flavour: Wide shallow pans stretch toward the estuary's edge, their surfaces crusted and still; the harvest is over and the flat-boats are beached, hull-up, along the near bank.

checkpoints.label: When did Tessek Oravi die?
answer_options: Between the evening meal and the second bell
Between the second bell of night and first light

checkpoints.label: Why was Tessek Oravi killed?
answer_options: Silencing a witness to an old crime
Preventing exposure of falsified trade accounts

clues.text: Examining the body at [loc:salt_works], the investigator shifts the outer robe aside and finds a narrow puncture wound on the left side of Tessek Oravi's torso; the robe itself has a corresponding slit in the fabric, its edges stiff with dried dark brown residue.

[char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that when she first knelt beside the body, she noticed a thin dark line running along the left side of Tessek Oravi's robe — too straight and too narrow to be a fold or a shadow — which she noted before anyone else disturbed the scene.

At the edge of the salt-pan platform where the body was found, Tessek Oravi's hands carry a visible coating of wet salt residue, and the railing she is slumped against faces directly onto the nearest brine channel.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The wet salt on the victim's hands suggests she may have fallen or been pushed into the brine channel, pointing toward drowning — but the residue was transferred when the body was placed against the railing, not from any contact with the water.

clues.text: [char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that [time:during the deep part of the night], while she was on watch at [loc:upper_walkway], she heard a single heavy thud from the direction of the Matron's complex — loud enough to make her stop and listen, though she heard nothing after it.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The thud heard by the night-watcher suggests an impact event — consistent with someone being struck or a body falling — but the sound was in fact Oravi being moved and the body being set down against the railing.

clues.text: [char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that in the weeks before her death, [char:Tessek Oravi] had complained to him of persistent stomach pains and had twice visited the herbalist who keeps a stall at [loc:covered_market].
clues.red_herring_explanation: The recent herbalist visits and stomach complaints suggest Oravi may have been experiencing symptoms of slow poisoning, but her illness was unrelated — a seasonal complaint — and distract from the physical evidence of stabbing.

clues.text: On the inner face of the [loc:records_room] doorframe, at roughly waist height, there is a rust-brown smear shaped like a partial hand-wipe — the heel of a palm pressed and then dragged downward across the wood.

[char:Nitha Tessek] tells the investigator that she herself locked the [loc:records_room] door the previous evening before retiring, and when she checked the following morning, the door stood unlocked and the interior bolt had been forced out of its bracket.

[char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that [time:in the middle of the night] she heard footsteps on the boards of [loc:upper_walkway] moving in the direction of the storage eaves, followed by a scraping sound she could not identify.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The footsteps and scraping on the covered walkway suggest a struggle or incident there, but the sounds were in fact Maret Doun moving the body along the walkway route — the Upper Walkway was traversed, not the site of the killing.

clues.text: [char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that [time:late in the evening], he saw [char:Tessek Oravi] walking in the direction of [loc:covered_market] with a ledger tucked under one arm, and she did not acknowledge him when he called out to her.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Dauvek's account places the victim heading toward the Covered Market late in the evening, suggesting that is where she went — but Oravi was in fact heading to the Records Room, which is accessible through the same route, and Dauvek misread her direction.

clues.text: [char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that [time:earlier that same evening], [char:Tessek Oravi] had told her she intended to check a cargo manifest in the dockside ledger office before the night was out.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Oravi's stated intention to visit the dockside office suggests the Quayside as a possible location for the killing, but she never went — she changed her plans and went to the Records Room instead.

clues.answer: Between the second bell of night and first light
clues.text: [char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that she personally watched [char:Tessek Oravi] enter the Matron's complex [time:at the second bell of night], and that by [time:one bell before first light], when Senne's watch ended, Oravi had not come back out through the main entrance.

clues.answer: Between the second bell of night and first light
clues.text: The open page of the [loc:records_room] ledger carries a final entry dated [time:the night of the death], written in the same careful hand as the surrounding columns; the ink in the rightmost column is smeared in a broad streak across the figures, as if the page was sharply disturbed before the ink had finished drying.

[char:Maret Doun] tells the investigator that she saw [char:Tessek Oravi] at [loc:salt_works] [time:in the late afternoon], and that Oravi had seemed agitated and distracted, speaking in clipped sentences and leaving before their conversation was finished.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Maret's account of Oravi's visible agitation in the late afternoon implies something may have happened to her then, but the distress was due to what Oravi had found in the old records — not the time of her death.

clues.answer: Between the evening meal and the second bell
clues.text: [char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that he encountered [char:Tessek Oravi] [time:just after the evening meal] and that she was in good health — speaking clearly, moving quickly — which was the last time he saw her alive.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Dauvek frames his sighting as the last confirmed sighting of Oravi alive, which implies she may have died shortly afterward — between the meal and the second bell — but she survived well past that point.

clues.text: [char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that when she first saw the body at sunrise, the color of the exposed skin still appeared to her as relatively fresh — she had assumed at the time that the death must have occurred very recently, perhaps within the hour.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Irret's lay impression that the body appeared fresh at sunrise leads her to estimate death just before or after first light, but she is not trained in such assessments, and the cool foggy morning had slowed the visible signs of death.

clues.text: When shown the key, [char:Maret Doun] states that she found it lying on the floor inside the [loc:records_room] doorway the morning after the death and had kept it intending to return it to the Matron; when the investigator asks when she entered the Records Room to find it, Maret pauses and then says she found it from the doorway, without going in.

The key fits the [loc:records_room] lock exactly; on the exterior face of the lock plate, there are three fresh scratches radiating from the keyhole — bright metal, not yet tarnished — consistent with a key inserted in poor light or in haste.

[char:Nitha Tessek] tells the investigator that she was in the corridor outside the [loc:records_room] late that same evening, and shows a tear along the hem of her ceremonial sash — the kind of snag, she says, that happens on the iron bracket beside the Records Room door.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The torn sash places Nitha Tessek physically near the Records Room that evening and establishes she was in the corridor — but the tear occurred during a routine earlier pass through the corridor, not during any attack.

clues.text: [char:Dauvek Pol] shows the investigator his own salt-scraper kept beneath his market counter — a short iron-bladed tool with a dark reddish-brown stain across the flat of the blade that he says is residue from a red-clay ceremonial salt he handles.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The dark stain on Dauvek's scraper resembles dried blood and puts a potentially bladed weapon in his possession, but the residue is in fact from red-clay salt — a common market commodity — and the scraper shows no signs of recent cleaning.

clues.text: When the investigator examines the [loc:upper_walkway] watch-log tablet with [char:Senne Arak] present, she identifies a gap of roughly [time:forty minutes] around [time:the third bell of night] containing no notched entries; asked to explain it, Senne says she stepped inside briefly to warm her hands and lost track of the time.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The unlogged gap in Senne's watch record creates a window during which she cannot account for her movements, suggesting she may have left her post to commit or assist in the crime — but the gap is in fact due to Senne falling briefly asleep at her post, a dereliction she is concealing out of fear of punishment.

clues.answer: Silencing a witness to an old crime
clues.text: On the open page of the [loc:records_room] ledger, among the columns of trade figures, a line from twelve years prior records a large payment to 'M. Doun, salt-works' with the notation 'compensation — structural loss, east pan'; beside it, in a different ink that appears freshly applied, a notation in a careful hand reads 'cross-ref: fire incident, Arak lineage stores.'

clues.answer: Silencing a witness to an old crime
clues.text: When shown the ledger entry, [char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that twelve years ago, a fire destroyed the Arak lineage's salt stores at the height of the harvest season; a young male laborer was found dead inside the storage building, and though the fire was recorded as accidental, there had been talk at the time — never formally pursued — that the blaze was set deliberately.

[char:Nitha Tessek] tells the investigator that [char:Tessek Oravi] had blocked the re-registration of a Tessek lineage property on three separate occasions over the past decade, and that the dispute had grown into an open enmity between them that everyone in [loc:matrons_house] knew about.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Nitha's openly stated hatred for Oravi and the specific, repeated nature of the property dispute provide a clear personal motive — but the feud, however genuine, was not the reason for the killing.

clues.answer: Preventing exposure of falsified trade accounts
clues.text: [char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that his aunt had, in his view, been quietly adjusting the recorded salt-weights in the Tessek ledgers for years in her own favor, and says that if someone had finally found proof of it, that person would have had reason to act before she could destroy the evidence.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Dauvek implies that Oravi was herself guilty of fraud and that her killer acted to prevent her from destroying evidence — an inversion that redirects suspicion and obscures the actual motive.

clues.text: [char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that [time:some weeks before the death], she overheard [char:Tessek Oravi] in a sharp exchange with someone she could not see, in which Oravi said clearly: 'those records are not yours to take, and they never will be.'
clues.red_herring_explanation: The overheard argument about records 'not being yours to take' suggests the motive was theft or appropriation of inheritance documents — consistent with Dauvek's known pressure on Oravi — but the exchange was in fact about Oravi refusing to let anyone tamper with the historical trade records she was reviewing.

leads.text: A traveling salt-auditor named [char:Irret Sou] was berthed at [loc:quayside] when the body was found — she reached it before anyone else did, and she mentioned to the runner who summoned help that something about how the body lay had struck her as wrong.

The victim [char:Tessek Oravi] had been sent by the Matron to review ledgers at [loc:records_room] that same evening — the room was found unlocked the following morning, in violation of standing orders that [char:Nitha Tessek] herself enforces.

The night-watcher [char:Senne Arak] holds her post at [loc:upper_walkway], which overlooks the passage between the Matron's House and the salt-works — she would have been on watch during the hours in question.

opening_narrative:
The boat from the outer estuary reaches [loc:quayside] in the grey hour before the salt-fog fully lifts. [loc:quayside] smells of brine and wet rope, and the flat-bottomed trade vessels knock quietly against the pilings as the investigator steps ashore. The runner who came to fetch him said only that a woman was dead at the salt-works, that the body had been found at dawn, and that the Tessek lineage had sent for someone from outside because no one in [loc:quayside] could be seen to take a side.

The dead woman is [char:Tessek Oravi], bookkeeper and salt-merchant of the Tessek lineage — a position of no small consequence in a town whose livelihood rises and falls with the tidal harvest. She was found [time:at sunrise] by the overseer, [char:Maret Doun], slumped against the railing of the main salt-pan platform at [loc:salt_works]. The Matron, [char:Nitha Tessek], has not yet come down from [loc:matrons_house]. The market is still shuttered. Word has spread anyway, as word always does in Vaur Tessek, carried on the same salt-wind that carries everything else.

The investigator has been told that [char:Tessek Oravi] was working alone the previous evening and that nothing seemed amiss. He has been told this by the same people who are now waiting to see what he will do with what he finds.
epilogue:
What [char:Tessek Oravi] had been assembling in the months before her death was not a formal accusation but a private record — a set of marginal notations in the old trade ledgers that, taken together, would have been difficult to ignore. She had never been a woman who made claims she could not support in ink, and she had not intended to bring anything before [char:Nitha Tessek] until every line was legible and every figure cross-referenced. She was not quite finished when [char:Maret Doun] learned what she was doing.

[char:Maret Doun] had built twelve years of quiet on the assumption that the fire at the Arak lineage stores had been closed as an accident and forgotten. The young laborer who died in it — a boy of seventeen named Pek Arak, her sister's son — had been working alone in the storage building when Maret set the blaze from outside, believing it empty. The payment recorded in the ledger as 'structural compensation' had in fact been a quieting sum arranged between Maret and a Tessek lineage factor who had since died, keeping the transaction out of any formal record. It was only Oravi's methodical habit of cross-referencing every unusual payment against the event records that had surfaced it at all. [char:Maret Doun] heard of it from a junior clerk who had noticed Oravi's notations and mentioned them to the overseer out of ordinary professional deference. Maret used the salt-scraper — a new one, drawn from the back stores so it would leave no traceable history — and she used the walkway she had walked every morning for twelve years to move the body before [loc:quayside] came to life.

[char:Senne Arak], whose family name matched the lineage of the boy who died, had no connection to the crime and no knowledge of it — her gap in the watch log was nothing more than exhaustion and the cold. [char:Dauvek Pol] lost his petition to revise the lineage document; [char:Nitha Tessek], to her visible displeasure, was required to honor [char:Tessek Oravi]'s last completed set of records as the authoritative version. [char:Irret Sou] completed her cargo audit and sailed south [time:before the next tide], leaving behind a single page of notes on the state of Vaur Tessek's trade records — thorough, precise, and filed where anyone diligent enough to look would eventually find it.
